title: Mock data in production caused by incomplete script implementation
description: Daily meeting prep digest was generating fabricated meeting data because the Python script contained hardcoded mock data in functions that were meant to call real APIs. The scheduled task executed the script directly instead of having Zo interpret instructions and call APIs.
context: Script had stub functions `fetch_calendar_events()` and `fetch_email_history()` that returned hardcoded mock data (always the same fake 'Aniket Partnership Follow-up' meeting). This was developer placeholder code that was never replaced with real API calls before being deployed to production via scheduled tasks.
outcome: Root cause identified through forensic investigation. Fixed by: (1) Updating scheduled task to use LLM-based execution where Zo calls APIs directly, (2) Deprecating the broken script, (3) Manually generating correct digest with real data. Scheduled task now works correctly.
key_insight: Scripts with stub functions should never reach production. LLM-based scheduled tasks that call APIs directly are more reliable than scripts with incomplete implementations. Always verify data sources in automated systems.

title: User-driven root cause analysis reveals system architecture gaps
description: User asked 'why isn't cleanup functionality addressing doc cleanup?' which revealed a legitimate architecture gap: no automation exists to detect stale documentation references to deprecated files.
context: After fixing the digest issue, 7 documentation files still referenced the deprecated script. User questioned why cleanup automation didn't catch this. Investigation revealed that weekly cleanup only handles old files (temp files, logs, exports) but doesn't validate documentation references.
outcome: Identified architecture gap, analyzed current cleanup systems, designed new 'Documentation Reference Validator' component. Created complete task package for implementation in new thread. User's question led to legitimate system improvement opportunity.
key_insight: User questions about 'why doesn't X work' often reveal real architectural gaps, not user misunderstanding. When a user asks why automation doesn't handle something, investigate whether it's a legitimate missing capability. Good automation should scale with system growth.

title: Running conversation-end script before manual Phase 0 lesson extraction
description: Executed the conversation-end script immediately when user said 'n5 conversation-end', but the command documentation explicitly states Phase 0 (Lesson Extraction) must be done BY THE LLM manually BEFORE running the script. The script cannot extract lessons because it lacks full conversation context that only the LLM has.
context: Command file states: 'Phase 0: Lesson Extraction (If Significant) - Performed by: The LLM directly (before running script)'. The workflow is: (1) LLM analyzes conversation for significant lessons, (2) LLM writes lessons to pending/, (3) THEN run the script. I skipped step 1-2 and went straight to running the script, causing incomplete execution.
outcome: User corrected me, saying 'Load the exact command and follow the exact instructions.' Had to go back, read the command file properly, extract lessons manually, then commit them separately. Lost time and required user intervention to correct.
key_insight: When executing commands, ALWAYS read the full command documentation first before taking action. Don't assume you know the workflow from memory. Phase 0 activities that require full conversation context must be done by the LLM before delegating to scripts. Scripts don't have conversation context - only the LLM does.
